output.summary: Heavyweight — full panel rewrite with design rethinking. CEO wants brainstorm on content/naming/structure.
Read current OpsPanel.tsx (506 lines), types.ts, SidePanel.tsx, vision.md, dashboard-store.ts. Current panel built around dead categories/features/backlogs model.

Available data: sessions, directiveState, activeDirectives, directiveHistory, teams, tasksBySession, workState (features, backlogs, conductor, index), sessionActivities, connected. Other panels cover: Team (agents), Tasks (directives+projects), Log (activity feed).

Unanimous: rename to Status, kill dead features/backlogs model, build on activeDirectives+sessions. 4 sections: System Vitals KPI, Active Directives with pipeline progress, Velocity (7d throughput), Session Health bar.
Single project: status-panel-rewrite (P0, moderate). Riley builds, Sarah reviews. Full rewrite + 7-file rename.
22 rename items across 7 files. All data available in existing store. No server changes needed.
2 tasks decomposed: rename-ops-to-status (22 items across 6 files) and build-status-panel (4 sections: Vitals, Active Directives, Velocity, Session Health). Sequential execution.
Using existing 'game' branch. No worktree needed.
Both tasks completed. Task 1: 22 rename items across 6 files. Task 2: StatusPanel.tsx created (503 lines) with 4 sections, OpsPanel.tsx deleted. TypeScript compiles clean.

Code review found 2 issues in useBadgeCounts.ts: (1) stale 'Ops' JSDoc comment, (2) dead workState.features badge logic. Both fixed — badge now counts from activeDirectives (failed steps). Re-verified: tsc clean, zero leftover 'ops' references.
All 9 DOD criteria met. StatusPanel replaces OpsPanel with system overview: vitals, active directives, velocity, session health. Dead features/backlogs code removed from both panel and badge logic.
